= Chapter 7: Governance, Roadmap, and Long-Term Ownership

== 7.1 End-State Ownership Model

This final chapter defines how Kosha should be operated after handover so feature velocity does not degrade system trust.

The ownership model has three concurrent responsibilities:

. Keep financial correctness and mutation integrity stable.
. Evolve product signals intentionally instead of adding redundant UI noise.
. Ship releases through deterministic verification gates.

Operational anchors:

. Release process baseline in link:{repo-blob-url}/README.md#L310[README.md].
. Contribution and branch policy in link:{repo-blob-url}/README.md#L337[README.md] and link:{repo-blob-url}/README.md#L356[README.md].
. Operational script inventory in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/README.md#L5[scripts/README.md].

'''

== 7.2 Source-of-Truth Registry

Use this map to avoid conflicting decisions across docs and code.

|===
| Domain | Primary Source | What It Controls
| Platform and setup | link:{repo-blob-url}/README.md#L1[README.md] | Environment, setup, deployment, contributor workflow
| Schema contract | link:{repo-blob-url}/README.md#L174[README.md] and link:{repo-blob-url}/supabase/schema.sql[supabase/schema.sql] | Idempotent DB shape, RLS, RPC availability
| Runtime scripts | link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/README.md#L5[scripts/README.md] | Ops/test script intent and QA guidance
| Release gate sequence | link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs#L3[scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs] | Ordered release checks and retries
| Deploy readiness | link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs#L58[scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs] | Live schema and RPC contract verification
| UX and page intent | link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md#L3[docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md] | Page purpose, consistency rules, quality gates
| Card signal governance | link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/CARD_SIGNAL_BLUEPRINT.md#L7[docs/CARD_SIGNAL_BLUEPRINT.md] | Focus-vs-deep card boundaries by page
| Chart governance | link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/CHART_INVENTORY_ROADMAP.md#L5[docs/CHART_INVENTORY_ROADMAP.md] | Chart type selection, redundancy decisions, roadmap order
| Release history | link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/changelog.js#L1[src/lib/changelog.js] | Human-readable release narrative and shipped scope
| Runtime crash telemetry | link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/runtimeMonitor.js#L64[src/lib/runtimeMonitor.js] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/errors/GlobalErrorBoundary.jsx#L8[src/components/errors/GlobalErrorBoundary.jsx] | Error capture, crash diagnostics, bug-report prefill
|===

Governance rule:

. If two artifacts disagree, update the source-of-truth artifact first, then align implementation.

'''

== 7.3 Product Signal Governance

Kosha should evolve by improving decision quality, not by increasing visual volume.

== 7.3.1 Card-level governance

Card signal boundaries are defined by page in link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/CARD_SIGNAL_BLUEPRINT.md#L7[docs/CARD_SIGNAL_BLUEPRINT.md], link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/CARD_SIGNAL_BLUEPRINT.md#L21[docs/CARD_SIGNAL_BLUEPRINT.md], link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/CARD_SIGNAL_BLUEPRINT.md#L34[docs/CARD_SIGNAL_BLUEPRINT.md], and link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/CARD_SIGNAL_BLUEPRINT.md#L55[docs/CARD_SIGNAL_BLUEPRINT.md].

Noise removal policy is explicit in link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/CARD_SIGNAL_BLUEPRINT.md#L66[docs/CARD_SIGNAL_BLUEPRINT.md]:

. Focus cards must be action-first.
. Deep cards should stay diagnostic and investigatory.
. Duplicate signal cards should be merged or removed.

== 7.3.2 Chart-level governance

Chart decisions are locked by matrix and roadmap in link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/CHART_INVENTORY_ROADMAP.md#L15[docs/CHART_INVENTORY_ROADMAP.md] and link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/CHART_INVENTORY_ROADMAP.md#L62[docs/CHART_INVENTORY_ROADMAP.md].

Execution rule is codified in link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/CHART_INVENTORY_ROADMAP.md#L115[docs/CHART_INVENTORY_ROADMAP.md]:

. Every chart must answer a unique decision question.
. Chart type must be minimal for the question.
. At least one actionable interpretation must exist.
. Redundancy flag should remain None or Low after review.

== 7.3.3 Reconciliation signal quality

Reconciliation quality model should be treated as a strategic trust signal, not only a page feature.

Core signal engines:

. Candidate/queue composition in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/reconciliation.js#L46[src/lib/reconciliation.js].
. Confidence drift detection in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/reconciliationMetrics.js#L29[src/lib/reconciliationMetrics.js].
. Alias demotion and cooldown quality mechanisms in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/reconciliationMetrics.js#L76[src/lib/reconciliationMetrics.js] and link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/reconciliationMetrics.js#L189[src/lib/reconciliationMetrics.js].

'''

== 7.4 Change Lifecycle: Idea to Production

Use this lifecycle for any non-trivial change.

. Frame the decision problem.
. Select governing artifact(s) to update first.
. Implement code changes in bounded modules.
. Run required verification scripts.
. Update release narrative.
. Ship through release-candidate gate.

Detailed operational contract:

. Contribution branch and commit policy in link:{repo-blob-url}/README.md#L337[README.md].
. Local pre-PR checks in link:{repo-blob-url}/README.md#L348[README.md].
. Release-candidate automation in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs#L3[scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs].
. Production smoke/testing guidance in link:{repo-blob-url}/README.md#L279[README.md].

'''

== 7.5 Playbooks by Change Type

== 7.5.1 Schema or RPC changes

. Apply schema changes idempotently via `supabase/schema.sql`.
. Re-run deploy readiness to verify tables/columns/RPCs.
. Re-run reconciliation schema live checks if related.

References:

. Schema idempotency note in link:{repo-blob-url}/README.md#L174[README.md].
. Deploy readiness probes in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs#L58[scripts/ops/check_deploy_readiness.mjs].
. Reconciliation schema live check in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/tests/test_reconciliation_schema_live.mjs#L50[scripts/tests/test_reconciliation_schema_live.mjs].

== 7.5.2 Financial mutation flow changes

. Preserve optimistic update plus rollback contract.
. Keep audit logging intact for all mutation actions.
. Run mutation stress and rollback contract checks.

References:

. Rollback contract assertions in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/tests/test_mutation_rollback_contract.mjs#L24[scripts/tests/test_mutation_rollback_contract.mjs].
. Stress lifecycle in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/tests/test_mutation_stress.mjs#L70[scripts/tests/test_mutation_stress.mjs].
. Audit action map in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/auditLog.js#L3[src/lib/auditLog.js].

== 7.5.3 Dashboard/Monthly/Analytics signal changes

. Update card blueprint scope if signal class changes.
. Update chart matrix/roadmap if chart purpose or redundancy status changes.
. Ensure page still satisfies first-viewport action gate.

References:

. Card blueprint in link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/CARD_SIGNAL_BLUEPRINT.md#L7[docs/CARD_SIGNAL_BLUEPRINT.md].
. Chart roadmap in link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/CHART_INVENTORY_ROADMAP.md#L62[docs/CHART_INVENTORY_ROADMAP.md].
. UX quality gates in link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md#L117[docs/PRODUCT_UX_PLAYBOOK.md].

== 7.5.4 Reconciliation model changes

. Validate queue and matching behavior end-to-end.
. Re-check drift and alias-quality outputs.
. Run reconciliation flow and metrics tests.

References:

. Reconciliation flow script in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/tests/test_reconciliation_flow.mjs#L1[scripts/tests/test_reconciliation_flow.mjs].
. Metrics engine in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/reconciliationMetrics.js#L5[src/lib/reconciliationMetrics.js].

== 7.5.5 Runtime reliability and bug pipeline changes

. Keep startup runtime monitor wiring intact.
. Keep global error boundary diagnostics handoff intact.
. Verify bug submit RPC and notify invocation paths.

References:

. Monitor startup in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/main.jsx#L9[src/main.jsx].
. Boundary diagnostics prefill in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/components/errors/GlobalErrorBoundary.jsx#L26[src/components/errors/GlobalErrorBoundary.jsx].
. Bug submission RPC in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/ReportBug.jsx#L184[src/pages/ReportBug.jsx].
. Notification invoke in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/ReportBug.jsx#L207[src/pages/ReportBug.jsx].

'''

== 7.6 Ownership Cadence

Use a fixed cadence to prevent drift.

== 7.6.1 Daily

. Monitor new bug report entries and crash spikes.
. Triage release blockers from open regressions.

== 7.6.2 Weekly

. Review reconciliation quality drift and alias behavior.
. Review Bills and Loans operational friction from support feedback.
. Verify roadmap sequence still matches product priorities.

Cadence hints in user-facing guide:

. Weekly workflow cue in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Guide.jsx#L402[src/pages/Guide.jsx].
. Monthly workflow cue in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/pages/Guide.jsx#L403[src/pages/Guide.jsx].

== 7.6.3 Monthly

. Run full release-candidate check on the current mainline.
. Confirm changelog reflects shipped behavior accurately.
. Reassess chart/card redundancy decisions.

References:

. Release candidate pass condition in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs#L96[scripts/ops/release_candidate_check.mjs].
. Changelog registry in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/changelog.js#L1[src/lib/changelog.js].
. Chart redundancy section in link:{repo-blob-url}/docs/CHART_INVENTORY_ROADMAP.md#L49[docs/CHART_INVENTORY_ROADMAP.md].

== 7.6.4 Quarterly

. Perform architecture cleanup sprint for stale flows and legacy assumptions.
. Review archived script relevance and remove dead operational paths.

Reference: archived script policy in link:{repo-blob-url}/scripts/README.md#L32[scripts/README.md].

'''

== 7.7 Release Narrative Discipline

Release quality is not complete without narrative traceability.

Changelog discipline:

. Every shipped release updates link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/changelog.js#L1[src/lib/changelog.js].
. Keep entries concise and decision-impact oriented.
. Preserve version chronology and avoid mixing speculative roadmap text into shipped notes.

Current pattern examples:

. Latest release block in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/changelog.js#L3[src/lib/changelog.js].
. Prior release continuity in link:{repo-blob-url}/src/lib/changelog.js#L14[src/lib/changelog.js].

'''

== 7.8 Final Handover Completion Checklist

This checklist marks transfer of ownership from build phase to sustain phase.

. Environment setup and deployment docs validated against current project.
. Release-candidate script passes on target branch.
. Deploy-readiness script passes against target Supabase project.
. Reconciliation schema and flow checks pass.
. Production asset integrity check passes for deployed URL.
. Changelog updated for latest shipped release.
. Card and chart governance docs aligned with current UI.
. Crash-report pipeline validated from app to bug report storage and optional webhook.

Primary command anchors:

. Script catalog in link:{repo-blob-url}/README.md#L246[README.md].
. Testing section in link:{repo-blob-url}/README.md#L279[README.md].
. Troubleshooting fallbacks in link:{repo-blob-url}/README.md#L382[README.md].

If all eight items are true, the system is considered handover-complete and sustainably operable.

'''

== 7.9 Closing Principle

Kosha should continue to optimize for trust per interaction, not feature count per release.

In practical terms:

. Correctness before cleverness.
. Signal quality before dashboard density.
. Deterministic release checks before deployment speed.
. Observability before blame.

This principle, combined with Chapters 1-7, is the full operating manual for ongoing ownership.
